title: SOC 2 CC5 — Change Impact Analysis Completeness for Production Releases

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOC 2 CC5.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.

value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: expects consideration of fraud and other changes — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Map Snow fields to your CMDB CI classes requiring analysis; (2) exclude standard changes via `chg_model=standard`; (3) integrate with CAB workflow to block closure in source system, Splunk mirrors drift; (4) monthly metric for % changes with complete analysis; (5) attach search export to change management KPI deck. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, state, u_impact_analysis, u_rollback_plan, u_security_review, start_date, cmdb_ci).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map Snow fields to your CMDB CI classes requiring analysis; (2) exclude standard changes via `chg_model=standard`; (3) integrate with CAB workflow to block closure in source system, Splunk mirrors drift; (4) monthly metric for % changes with complete analysis; (5) attach search export to change management KPI deck.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" state IN ("Implement","Closed") earliest=-60d
| eval missing=mvjoin(
    if(isnull(u_impact_analysis) OR u_impact_analysis="","impact",null()),
    if(isnull(u_rollback_plan) OR u_rollback_plan="","rollback",null()),
    if(isnull(u_security_review) OR u_security_review="false","sec_review",null()),
    ",")
| where missing!=""
| stats count by missing, cmdb_ci
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC5 — Change Impact Analysis Completeness for Production Releases** — CC5 expects consideration of fraud and other changes. Verifying every production change record carries impact analysis, rollback plan, and security review flags demonstrates change-related risks were evaluated before deployment.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, state, u_impact_analysis, u_rollback_plan, u_security_review, start_date, cmdb_ci). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:change_request", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **missing** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where missing!=""` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by missing, cmdb_ci** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**SOC 2 CC5 — Change Impact Analysis Completeness for Production Releases** — CC5 expects consideration of fraud and other changes. Verifying every production change record carries impact analysis, rollback plan, and security review flags demonstrates change-related risks were evaluated before deployment.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, state, u_impact_analysis, u_rollback_plan, u_security_review, start_date, cmdb_ci). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (defective changes), Bar chart (missing field counts), Single value (% complete inverse).
visualization: Table (defective changes), Bar chart (missing field counts), Single value (% complete inverse).
knownFalsePositives: Control test failures during audit windows, immature control rollouts, or known compensating-control gaps.
